checks.command: find tools -maxdepth 1 -type f | wc -l equals 4, all 4 contracted files present
skill.json valid JSON, no vendor/runtime identifiers (claude/anthropic/codex/openai/gpt)
exactly ONE SKILL.md in the whole repo (excluding .git)
no tools/.claude or tools/.codex fork directories
every tools/*.md has frontmatter with 6 required keys
tools/ contains exactly the 4 contracted files, no stray extras

adversarial_check.description: Two-part check beyond the 9 listed commands. (1) Runtime-adapter simulation: loaded tools/skill.json programmatically, confirmed its 'entry_point' field resolves to tools/SKILL.md on disk, confirmed all 10 capabilities[].module paths resolve to real files, then extracted every backtick-quoted path-like reference from SKILL.md's body (excluding fenced code blocks) and confirmed each resolves on disk as a file or directory as appropriate -- with two explicit, justified exceptions: NIZAM.json and registry/nizam-index.schema.json, both forward-references to content contracted for feature 006 per this contract's own non_goals, correctly still absent. All in-scope references resolve; zero unexplained dangling references. (2) DD-4 uniqueness negative test, conducted entirely in the scratchpad (never inside the repo): confirmed the real repo's 'find . -name SKILL.md -not -path ./.git/* | wc -l' yields 1, then built a simulated duplicate-fork tree solely under /tmp/claude-1000/.../scratchpad/dd4_sim/ (tools/SKILL.md copied to both tools/SKILL.md and tools/.duplicate-runtime-fork/SKILL.md) and re-ran the identical find|wc -l logic against that scratchpad tree, yielding 2 -- proving verification command 3 is load-bearing and would catch a genuine DD-4 violation, not a vacuous pass. The repo's own tools/ directory was confirmed unmodified (no .duplicate-runtime-fork present) after the simulation.
